entry.title: AFAB was locked because an asset was in use. How to unlock it for the next AFAB run ?.
entry.content: We have run AFAB at 6 PM yesterday but the program ran empty because one asset was in use.  What do we need to do to unlock AFAB again to allow a new run ?

entry.title: FWTR056 Error During NDF Transaction Creation in FTR_CREATE
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We are encountering error FWTR056 while attempting to create an NDF transaction using transaction FTR_CREATE.

 

We have performed a detailed review of the configuration, including product type, transaction type, and all relevant customizing settings, and no inconsistencies were identified.

 

The issue occurs consistently during transaction creation.

 

For your analysis, we have attached:

• A detailed step-by-step document outlining how the error is reproduced

• Screenshots and configuration details related to the product and transaction setup

 

At this point, we suspect there may be a missing dependency, configuration inconsistency not visible at first level, or a system-related issue.

 

Could you please assist us in identifying the root cause of error FWTR056 and advise on the appropriate resolution?

 

If additional information is required, we will be happy to provide it.

 

Thank you for your support.

 

Best regards,


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: None of these


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Need All Characters in Uppercase in DMEE Payment File (PAYM – /ZGA_CGI_XML_CT_CITI)
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We require all alphanumeric characters in the payment file to appear in capital letters, as highlighted in the attached example.

We attempted to achieve this using the conversion function CLU in the DMEE tree, however, the system is generating an incorrect payment file after applying this function.

Request your guidance on how we can correctly convert the values to uppercase within the DMEE format tree:

  • Tree Type: PAYM
  • Format Tree: /ZGA_CGI_XML_CT_CITI

Could you please advise which conversion function is required.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,
Gokul Sawant

entry.title: Asset Report not executed in ECC
entry.content: When running the asset reports in the system, an error message appears stating ‘Fiscal year change has not yet been made for company code XXX,’ even though the fiscal year is open for the asset period in OAAQ (1+2). The detailed screenshots are attached herewith for your reference. Kindly advise on how this issue can be resolved.

entry.title: F17P cannot consolidate balance reports across multiple company codes (Ford Blue, Pro, and Model-e).
entry.content:
Currently, Ford utilizes three distinct company codes to represent its business segments: Ford Blue, Ford Pro, and Ford Model-e. Many customers conduct business across all three segments simultaneously.

When the Balance Confirmation process is executed using T-code F17P specifically for Poland and CZECH, the system generates individual reports restricted to a single company code. This results in fragmented reporting for a single customer entity. There is a business requirement to provide these customers with a single, unified balance confirmation statement that aggregates their financial position across all relevant Ford company codes, rather than receiving three separate documents.

Solution Needed Expert guidance is requested from the SAP support teams on the following:

  1. Standard SAP Functionality: Is there a standard configuration or parameter within F17P (or related programs like RFKORB00/RFDORB00) that allows  to generate consolidated report for multiple company code for a single customer?
  2. BTE (Business Transaction Events): Can a specific BTE (such as those in the 1310 or 1320 se

entry.title: SAP ECC 6.0 – Fiscal Year Variant Change ( 5-4-4 → Calendar Month)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently reviewing a Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) change in SAP ECC 6.0 and would appreciate guidance from who have handled similar scenarios.

Current Situation

  • Financial year: 1 July → 30 June
  • Existing FY variant is year‑dependent
  • Period 12 ends on 26 June, not 30 June (as per 5-4-4 method)
  • Dates 27–30 June are posted to next financial year (Period 1)
  • This early‑close model was historically implemented (likely for operational / RE‑FX reasons)
  • The setup is used across FI, Asset Accounting, MM, SD, RE‑FX, CO, BW, payroll posting, and custom jobs

New Business Requirement

Business is planning an SAP ECC → SAP S/4HANA conversion. Therefore, business want to align its fiscal calendar to calendar month end periods prior to S/4HANA migration. (from 5-4-4 to Calendar month)

(27–30 June 2026 must remain in the current FY, not roll into the next one)

From our understanding, this cannot be achieved without changing fiscal period boundaries in OB29.

Questions to the Community

  1. In SAP ECC 6.0, how risky is it in practice to modify an existing year‑dependent FY variant with historical postings?
  2. Has anyone successfully modified an existing FY variant (instead of creating a new one) without later issues?
  3. Are there any SAP‑supported alternatives to include 27–30 June in the same FY without redefining the FYV?
  4. From an audit/governance perspective, which approach have you found easier to defend?
  5. Any specific Asset Accounting, BW or RE‑FX pitfalls we should be aware of?

We are looking for real‑world ECC experience, not just OB29 theory.

Any guidance, lessons learned, or relevant SAP Notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Account Determination MM - How to deactivate rules
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have noticed that on S4/HANA Public Cloud it doesn't seem to be possible to deactivate rules in MM account determination once they have been activated:

System does not allow to deselect these checkboxes although valuation class entries have not been maintained. Is there a way to deactivate these rules? If not, this would mean that you need to maintain account determination for all valuation classes in this example. 

Thanks for your input in advance and best regards,

Max

entry.title: Event-Based Revenue Recognition - How to consider tax (VAT)? (Scope Item 3M3)
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are on S/4HANA Public cloud and with regard to EBRR for Service documents (service contracts and service orders), we are using revenue recognition key CCOCF and CCS.

In case we have costs on a service order and revnues have not been billed, system creates a accrual posting for not-billed revenues which leads to realization of revenue in same period like costs were posted. I would have expected that there should be also a posting which considering tax (VAT) but this is not happening. Tax posting is happening when invoice is issued to the customer. But from our point of view taxes need to be posted as soon as revenue is realized. Is this a standard behaviour of the system or are there missing settings in configuration? if this is standard system behaviour, are there any workarounds how this can be solved? Would be great if someone can give some insights.

Thanks a lot for your input and best regards,

Max

entry.title: Deactive / sell / scrap assets in company codes closed for a long time
entry.content:
We want to delete all assets belong to companies had been sold/closed for a long time. Posting period are all closed for these companies. We tried tcodes ABAVN ABAON F-92 to remove these assets but they tried to create posting and all require company period open. Asset cannot be deleted once transaction exists either. Is there a way to scrap or delete them without any new posting? We just want to delete them from database so to clear FI_DOCUMNT errors and compress ACDOCA.

I also archived some assets. Even DELETION step is run, assets still in ANLA table. How do we delete these assets without creating any posting?

Thanks, Colin

entry.title: Recursive BOM: CK739 and CK740 error in CK11N for Recursive BOM
entry.content:
Hello Everyone

 

We have a BOM with Material: 512345, plant: 1023, BOM usage: 1, Alternative BOM: 1.

In this component items are 123456, 223456, 323456. We have costed this for this month using CK11N and the cost has been marked and released too. It is fine upto here.

 

From next month, we are trying to use Recursive BOM for 512345. So, we have created another BOM with Material: 512345, plant: 1023, BOM usage: 1, Alternative BOM: 2.

In this, we have given component item as 512345 (only one component). And we have ticked ‘Recursiveness Allowed’ for this component item in this BOM. I have given header item quantity as 10EA and component item quantity as 1EA.

And now doing CK11N for Material: 512345, plant: 1023, BOM usage: 1, Alternative BOM: 2 for the next month. But when I running this I am getting an error as below:

CK739  Costing for cycle 00001 has no solution

CK740  Material 5000061 in plant 1710 belongs to cycle 00001 costed with errors

I am able to see the looping inside CK11N and quantity is breaking down to zero. But the value everywhere is coming as zero. And also when I checked the quantity structure inside CK11N, it is clearing showing as BOM usage 1 and Alternative 2.

We are not using ‘Special Procurement’ concept in the material master.

Requesting you to somebody help on this. Moreover this is an urgent requirement.

 

 

#Recursive BOM #CK11N #CK740 #CK739 #Recursiveness Allowed

entry.title: Unable to Reverse Bill of Exchange Special G/L Document in F 20 – Error F5345
entry.content:
We are facing an issue while reversing a Bill of Exchange Special G/L transaction using T‑code F‑20. When we enter the correct Key Date (same as the due date), the system returns the following error:

Error Message: No due item exists at the key date (Message no. F5345)

Issue Details:

  • Transaction Type: Bill of Exchange – Special G/L
  • Reversal T‑code: F‑20
  • Key Date entered: Correct due date
  • System still shows error F5345
  • Other Special G/L transactions are reversing successfully
  • OB86 period is maintained as Period 5
  • Please find the attached error details document

entry.title: Automatic Account Determination - Value String - GOOD RECEIPT for Project Stock
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I'm struggling with Automatic Account Determination during good receipt for project stock for a material (value and quantity updated). I do not understand the Transaction/Event key that are being posted to during goods receipt.

Currently in OME9 the Account Assignment Category "Q" is configured like this:

So, accordingly to OMJJ, the value string WE06 should be used for this Automatic Account Determination:

Anyway, once i record the goods receipt, the Transaction/Eventy Key used are always the usual one (BSX and WRX) from WE01 i think.

I also tried to simulate the behaviour with OMWB simulation:

 

But if i choose the first "GR for project stock", then:

 

 

Does anyone know why ? 

 

Thank you for your help

entry.title: Error in Chart of Dep
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We have created the Chart of Depreciation: 1000 when we are trying to assign COD to company code :1000.

we are getting below issues:

Error -1: “Parallel crcy area (crcy type 30) missing for area 01 ch. of deprec. 1000”

Error -2: “Ledger NL defined in CoCd 1000 is not active in chart of depreciation 1000”

kindly provide the solution and let us know what the root cause of the issues is.

Please find my attachment.

Regards,

entry.title: Unable to generate a customer account statement in a PDF Form
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Greetings of the day!!!

As per SAP suggestion, we are using the report "RFKORD10" to send the customer account statements seperately for the Zero Balance Accounts and the Detailed Accont Statement.
However, when we send the statement, it is sending the statement in an SAP Script Format. Request you to kindly suggest if there is any way to send the statement in a PDF Form which we are currently using it from "T Code F.27"

Please refer to the earlier SAP Case "1394356/2025".

Thanks & Regards,
Sainath Manchala

entry.title: Vendor Details for RE document
entry.content:
Hi Team,

we had fetched FAGLL03H and FBL3N report for some GL and basis on which checking vendor details for those documents.

we had found that for RE document type vendor details is not coming in FAGLL03H and FBL3N both. 

attachment is there for reference for QA server.

Thanks

Monika 

9717721884

entry.title: Period Determination Issue in “Close Period for Material Master Records” with Custom FYV
entry.content:
Hi SAP expert, 

I would like to seek clarification regarding the app “Close Period for Material Master Records/Allow posting to previous period” in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

From my understanding, this app displays the current period, previous period, and last period of the previous year. However, I would like to better understand which SSCUI configuration controls the determination of these periods.

As an example, consider a scenario where a fiscal year variant (e.g. SZ) is defined as year-dependent, with fiscal years maintained for multiple years. Assume the fiscal year is structured as:

  • From 21 March YYYY to 20 March YYYY+1

For instance, for FY2025:

  • Period 01/2025 > Last day: 20 April 2025
  • Period 02/2025 > Last day: 20 May 2025
  • …
  • Up to Period 12/2025 > Last day: 20 March 2026

Based on this setup, the system will show 002/2025 as the current period in the apps' Close Period for Material Master Records.

Therefore, I would like to kindly ask:

  • Which SSCUI configuration is responsible for controlling the period determination in this app?
  • Is it solely driven by the Fiscal Year Variant, or are there additional configurations (e.g. MM-related settings) that influence the current period?

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP Flexible Workflow - Vendor Invoice - Initial Conditions
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to have a Workflow for AP invoices which had initial condition "Document Type". Some Vendors / Document Types do not need workflow.

Is this possible with Flexible Workflow F2783 ?

Flexible workflow considers all journal Entries which have a Vendor line item (in this case) ?

Kind Regards

entry.title: Anlagengitter mit einzeiliger Darstellung notwendig
entry.content:
Es fehlt die Funtkon, das Anlagengitter S_ALR_87011990 einzeilig darzustellen.

Die Gitterversion bietet nicht so viele Spalten.

entry.title: criar recebimentos por conta
entry.content:
Olá,

Na tela da Contas a Receber, há a opção de Pagamento por conta. Onde configuro para quando for realizar a baixa de uma nota fiscal de saída com imposto retido, esse campo já venha preenchido para que a baixa da nota seja feita no valor bruto da nota?

entry.title: How Ghana’s Local Content Enforcement Is Changing SAP S/4HANA Solution Design in Oil & Gas
entry.content:
The gap between what sellers think local content means and how it is implemented in Ghana’s oil and gas sector is widening.

In February 2026, the Petroleum Commission of Ghana reiterated its push toward deeper local-content enforcement.

Under Ghana’s local content framework (L.I. 2204 and subsequent amendments), critical operational scopes are now reserved for Indigenous Ghanaian Companies (IGCs), with stricter expectations around equity participation, partnerships, and reporting.

This is no longer a broad policy decision.
It is enforced through structure and proof.

Higher equity barriers: while 5% equity is the floor for petroleum licenses, providing goods and services via joint venture often requires at least 10% IGC participation.

Strategic alliances are being enforced: foreign firms are increasingly pushed into partnerships to demonstrate real technology transfer, not passive ownership.

The evidence trail matters: reporting expectations in 2026 increasingly require auditable proof of in-country spend and local participation.

For enterprise sellers working on S/4HANA programs in Ghana’s upstream sector, this is starting to show up as a design constraint across the system landscape, not only a compliance checkbox.
 
Across S/4HANA (Finance, Procurement, Projects) and the Oil & Gas industry solution, requirements are becoming more explicit:
• Vendor master data must classify and validate Indigenous Ghanaian Company (IGC) status, ownership, and eligibility for reserved scopes
• Joint venture and partner structures must reflect enforceable equity participation, not just commercial agreements
• Procurement and contract allocation must be traceable to demonstrate compliance with reserved operational scopes
• Project and operational spend must distinguish in-country vs external value with audit-level precision
• Data lineage across procurement, projects and finance must be provable, not reconstructed
 
This is starting to surface early in deal cycles during solution design, data model validation, and compliance workshops not just post-implementation.
 
If you’re running SAP S/4HANA deals in Ghana’s upstream sector, are you accounting for how local content requirements will be modelled, tracked, and defended across your solution design? Has local content started to affect your deal timelines or how your solution is being evaluated?
